title: The Well-Architected Framework
description:
## The Six Pillars

The **AWS Well-Architected Framework** is the lens architects use to review workloads and justify trade-offs. SAP-C02 questions are frequently really asking *"which pillar does the requirement prioritize, and what design follows?"* There are **six** pillars.

| Pillar | Core idea | One-line design implication |
|---|---|---|
| **Operational Excellence** | Run and monitor systems; improve processes. | Automate operations as code (IaC, runbooks) and design for observability and small, reversible changes. |
| **Security** | Protect data, systems, and assets. | Apply least privilege, encrypt in transit and at rest, and use identity (roles, not keys) with strong audit (CloudTrail). |
| **Reliability** | Recover from failure; meet demand. | Design for failure across AZs/Regions, automate recovery, and test with backups and well-defined RTO/RPO. |
| **Performance Efficiency** | Use resources efficiently as demand changes. | Pick the right resource types, scale elastically, and adopt managed/serverless services to avoid undifferentiated work. |
| **Cost Optimization** | Avoid unnecessary cost. | Match supply to demand (auto scaling, Savings Plans/Spot), measure spend, and decommission waste. |
| **Sustainability** | Minimize environmental impact. | Maximize utilization, choose efficient instance families/Regions, and reduce the resources needed per unit of work. |

### Using the pillars

The pillars frequently **conflict**, and the architect's job is to make the trade-off explicit. Multi-Region active-active maximizes *Reliability* but hurts *Cost Optimization* and *Operational Excellence*; a single Spot fleet is great for *Cost* but poor for *Reliability* if the workload can't tolerate interruption.

> A useful exam habit: read the scenario, identify the **dominant requirement** ("must survive a Region outage", "must minimize steady-state cost", "must meet a strict compliance audit"), map it to a pillar, and let that drive the answer.

AWS provides the **Well-Architected Tool** in the console to run structured reviews against these pillars and track improvement items over time.
